disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Blunt head impact — fell ~1.5 m backward off a wall onto concrete, striking the back of the head 30 minutes ago
trigger_question_examples: What happened, how did he fall?
How far did he fall and what did he land on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He was up on a low wall, about a metre and a half, lost his balance and fell straight back onto the concrete. He hit the back of his head hard.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Witnessed loss of consciousness for about 2 minutes at the time of injury
trigger_question_examples: Was he knocked out? For how long?
Did he pass out when he fell?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, he was completely out cold for about two minutes. I couldn't wake him at first.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Post-traumatic amnesia and confusion — repeatedly asking what happened, not remembering the fall
trigger_question_examples: Is he confused, does he know what happened?
Is he asking the same things over and over?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He keeps asking me what happened, like every minute, and he doesn't remember falling at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Repeated forceful vomiting — three episodes in 15 minutes since the injury
trigger_question_examples: Has he been sick or vomited?
How many times has he thrown up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's thrown up three times now in the last little while, really forcefully, and he keeps retching.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Progressively worsening conscious level — increasingly drowsy, difficult to keep awake and rouse
trigger_question_examples: How awake is he right now, can you keep him talking?
Is he getting harder to wake up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's getting sleepier and sleepier. I have to keep shaking him to get a word out of him now, and it's worse than a few minutes ago.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: One pupil appears larger than the other on the roommate's video observation (right side)
trigger_question_examples: Do his pupils, the black centres of his eyes, look the same size?
You said one eye looks different — can you see the pupils?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, now that I look, the right one is definitely bigger than the left, and it's not really reacting to the light on my phone.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe, worsening headache localised to the back of the head with a large soft swelling at the impact site
trigger_question_examples: Is he complaining of a headache, and where?
Is there any swelling or a lump where he hit his head?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's holding the back of his head saying it really hurts, and there's a big soft lump back there where he landed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cannot sit or stand — severe dizziness and vomiting on any attempt to move upright
trigger_question_examples: Can he sit up or move at all?
What happens when he tries to get up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Every time he tries to sit up he gets really dizzy and throws up again, so he's just lying on the couch.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No known regular medications and no blood thinners, per roommate
trigger_question_examples: Does he take any regular medicines, especially blood thinners?
Any medical conditions you know of?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Not that I know of. I've never seen him take any tablets, and I don't think he's on blood thinners.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Possible alcohol involvement earlier in the day — roommate unsure whether he had been drinking
trigger_question_examples: Had he had any alcohol or anything to drink today?
Could anything else be affecting how he's behaving?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: We were out earlier so maybe he'd had a couple, I'm honestly not sure how much.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't really want to get him in trouble, but he might have had a few drinks.
